name: Cincinnati-Style Chili
recipeIngredient: Salt and fresh ground black pepper
kidney beans; grated Cheddar; chopped white onion -- and oyster crackers
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy pot, heat the oil over moderate heat. Cook the onions and garlic until softened.
2. Add the ground beef to the pot and stir until the beef is no longer pink. Remove any excess fat and discard it.
3. Add the chili powder, paprika, cumin, allspice, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, salt, and pepper to taste. Stir to combine and cook for 1 minute.
4. Add the bay leaf, red wine vinegar, molasses, tomato sauce, and water. Bring the mixture to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and simmer the chili, covered, for 1 1/2 hours. If necessary, add additional water to achieve a thick and soupy consistency.
6. Remove the bay leaf before serving. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as desired.
7. Serve the chili over cooked spaghetti and traditional accompaniments such as kidney beans, grated Cheddar cheese, chopped white onion, and oyster crackers.
